problems.promptEn: Practice 1.2. An investor enters into a short forward contract to sell 100,000 British pounds for U.S. dollars at an exchange rate of 1.3000 USD per pound. How much does the investor gain or lose if the exchange rate at the end of the contract is (a) 1.2900 and (b) 1.3200?
problems.promptZh: й—®йўҳ 1.2. жҠ•иө„иҖ…зӯҫи®ўдёҖд»Ҫз©әеӨҙиҝңжңҹеҗҲзәҰпјҢд»ҘжҜҸзЈ… 1.3000 зҫҺе…ғзҡ„жұҮзҺҮеҮәе”® 100,000 иӢұй•‘е…‘жҚўзҫҺе…ғгҖӮеҰӮжһңеҗҲзәҰз»“жқҹж—¶зҡ„жұҮзҺҮдёә (a) 1.2900 е’Ң (b) 1.3200пјҢжҠ•иө„иҖ…дјҡиҺ·еҲ©жҲ–жҚҹеӨұеӨҡе°‘пјҹ
problems.answer: See the official worked solution below.
problems.answerEn: See the official worked solution below.
problems.explanation:
1.2
(a) The investor is obligated to sell pounds for 1.3000 when they are worth 1.2900. The
gain is (1.3000вҲ’1.2900) Г—100,000 = USD 1,000.

(b) The investor is obligated to sell pounds for 1.3000 when they are worth 1.3200. The
loss is (1.3200вҲ’1.3000)Г—100,000 = USD 2,000

problems.promptEn: Practice 1.4. Suppose that you write a put contract with a strike price of USD 40 and an expiration date in 3 months. The current stock price is USD 41 and the contract is on 100 shares. What have you committed yourself to? How much could you gain or lose?
problems.promptZh: й—®йўҳ 1.4. еҒҮи®ҫдҪ еҚ–еҮәдёҖд»ҪзңӢи·ҢжңҹжқғеҗҲзәҰпјҢжү§иЎҢд»·ж јдёә 40 зҫҺе…ғпјҢеҲ°жңҹж—Ҙдёә 3 дёӘжңҲеҗҺгҖӮеҪ“еүҚиӮЎзҘЁд»·ж јдёә 41 зҫҺе…ғпјҢиҜҘеҗҲзәҰеҜ№еә” 100 иӮЎиӮЎзҘЁгҖӮдҪ жүҝиҜәдәҶд»Җд№ҲпјҹдҪ еҸҜиғҪиҺ·еҫ—жҲ–жҚҹеӨұеӨҡе°‘пјҹ
problems.answer: See the official worked solution below.
problems.answerEn: See the official worked solution below.
problems.explanation:
1.4
You have sold a put option. You have agreed to buy 100 shares for USD 40 per share if the party
on the other side of the contract chooses to exercise the right to sell for this price. The option
will be exercised only when the price of stock is below USD 40. Suppose, for example, that the
option is exercised when the price is USD 30. You have to buy at USD 40 shares that are worth USD 30;
you lose USD 10 per share, or USD 1,000 in total. If the option is exercised when the price is USD 20, you
lose USD 20 per share, or USD 2,000 in total. The worst that can happen is that the price of the stock
declines to almost zero during the three-month period. This highly unlikely event would cost
you USD 4,000. In return for the possible future losses, you receive the price of the option from
the purchaser.

problems.promptEn: Practice 1.5. You would like to speculate on a rise in the price of a certain stock. The current stock price is USD 29 and a 3-month call with a strike price of USD 30 costs USD 2.90. You have USD 5,800 to invest. Identify two alternative investment strategies, one in the stock and the other in an option on the stock. What are the potential gains and losses from each?
problems.promptZh: й—®йўҳ 1.5. дҪ еёҢжңӣжҠ•жңәжҹҗеҸӘиӮЎзҘЁд»·ж јдёҠж¶ЁгҖӮеҪ“еүҚиӮЎд»·дёә 29 зҫҺе…ғпјҢдёҖд»Ҫжү§иЎҢд»·ж јдёә 30 зҫҺе…ғгҖҒжңҹйҷҗдёә 3 дёӘжңҲзҡ„зңӢж¶Ёжңҹжқғд»·ж јдёә 2.90 зҫҺе…ғгҖӮдҪ жңү 5,800 зҫҺе…ғеҸҜжҠ•иө„гҖӮиҜ·з»ҷеҮәдёӨз§ҚеҸҜйҖүжҠ•иө„зӯ–з•ҘпјҡдёҖз§ҚжҠ•иө„иӮЎзҘЁпјҢеҸҰдёҖз§ҚжҠ•иө„иҜҘиӮЎзҘЁжңҹжқғгҖӮдёӨз§Қзӯ–з•Ҙеҗ„иҮӘзҡ„жҪңеңЁж”¶зӣҠе’ҢжҚҹеӨұжҳҜд»Җд№Ҳпјҹ
problems.answer: See the official worked solution below.
problems.answerEn: See the official worked solution below.
problems.explanation:
1.5
One strategy is to buy 200 shares. Another is to buy 2,000 call options. If the share price rises substantially, the option strategy gives larger gains because it is leveraged. For example, if the share price rises to USD 40, the option strategy gains 2,000 * (40 - 30) - 5,800 = USD 14,200, while the stock strategy gains 200 * (40 - 29) = USD 2,200. If the share price falls, the option strategy gives larger losses. For example, if the share price falls to USD 25, the stock strategy loses 200 * (29 - 25) = USD 800, while the option strategy loses the entire USD 5,800 investment. This illustrates the built-in leverage in options.
problems.explanationEn:
1.5

If the share price falls, the option strategy gives larger losses. For example, if the share price falls to USD 25, the stock strategy loses 200 * (29 - 25) = USD 800, while the option strategy loses the entire USD 5,800 investment. This illustrates the built-in leverage in options.

problems.promptEn: Practice 1.6. Suppose that you own 5,000 shares worth USD 25 each. How can put options be used to provide you with insurance against a decline in the value of your holding over the next 4 months?
problems.promptZh: й—®йўҳ 1.6. еҒҮи®ҫжӮЁжӢҘжңү 5,000 иӮЎжҜҸиӮЎд»·еҖј 25 зҫҺе…ғзҡ„иӮЎзҘЁгҖӮеҰӮдҪ•дҪҝз”ЁзңӢи·ҢжңҹжқғдёәжӮЁжҸҗдҫӣдҝқйҷ©пјҢйҳІжӯўжӮЁжүҖжҢҒиө„дә§еңЁжңӘжқҘ 4 дёӘжңҲеҶ…д»·еҖјдёӢи·Ңпјҹ
problems.answer: See the official worked solution below.
problems.answerEn: See the official worked solution below.
problems.explanation:
1.6
You could buy 50 put option contracts (each on 100 shares) with a strike price of USD 25 and an
expiration date in four months. If at the end of four months, the stock price proves to be less
than USD 25, you can exercise the options and sell the shares for USD 25 each.

problems.promptEn: Practice 1.7. When first issued, a stock provides funds for a company. Is the same true of a stock option? Discuss.
problems.promptZh: й—®йўҳ 1.7. йҰ–ж¬ЎеҸ‘иЎҢж—¶пјҢиӮЎзҘЁдёәе…¬еҸёжҸҗдҫӣиө„йҮ‘гҖӮиӮЎзҘЁжңҹжқғд№ҹжҳҜеҰӮжӯӨеҗ—пјҹи®Ёи®әгҖӮ
problems.answer: See the official worked solution below.
problems.answerEn: See the official worked solution below.
problems.explanation:
1.7
An exchange-traded stock option provides no funds for the company. It is a security sold by
one investor to another. The company is not involved. By contrast, a stock when it is first
issued, is sold by the company to investors and does provide funds for the company.
problems.explanationEn:
1.7
An exchange-traded stock option provides no funds for the company. It is a security sold by
one investor to another. The company is not involved. By contrast, a stock when it is first
issued, is sold by the company to investors and does provide funds for the company.

problems.promptEn: Practice 1.8. Explain why a futures contract can be used for either speculation or hedging.
problems.promptZh: й—®йўҳ 1.8. и§ЈйҮҠдёәд»Җд№Ҳжңҹиҙ§еҗҲзәҰеҸҜд»Ҙз”ЁдәҺжҠ•жңәжҲ–еҜ№еҶІгҖӮ
problems.answer: See the official worked solution below.
problems.answerEn: See the official worked solution below.
problems.explanation:
1.8
If a trader has an exposure to the price of an asset, a hedge with futures contracts can be used.
If the trader will gain when the price decreases and lose when the price increases, a long
futures position will hedge the risk. If the trader will lose when the price decreases and gain
when the price increases, a short futures position will hedge the risk. Thus, either a long or a
short futures position can be entered into for hedging purposes.
If the trader has no exposure to the price of the underlying asset, entering into a futures
contract is speculation. If the trader takes a long position, there is a gain when the assetвҖҷs
price increases and a loss when it decreases. If the trader takes a short position, there is a loss
when the assetвҖҷs price increases and a gain when it decreases.
problems.explanationEn:
1.8
If a trader has an exposure to the price of an asset, a hedge with futures contracts can be used.
If the trader will gain when the price decreases and lose when the price increases, a long
futures position will hedge the risk. If the trader will lose when the price decreases and gain
when the price increases, a short futures position will hedge the risk. Thus, either a long or a
short futures position can be entered into for hedging purposes.
If the trader has no exposure to the price of the underlying asset, entering into a futures
contract is speculation. If the trader takes a long position, there is a gain when the assetвҖҷs
price increases and a loss when it decreases. If the trader takes a short position, there is a loss
when the assetвҖҷs price increases and a gain when it decreases.

problems.promptEn: Practice 1.13. A company knows that it is due to receive a certain amount of a foreign currency in 4 months. What type of option contract is appropriate for hedging?

problems.answer: See the official worked solution below.
problems.answerEn: See the official worked solution below.
problems.explanation:
1.13
A long position in a four-month put option on the foreign currency can provide insurance
against the exchange rate falling below the strike price. It ensures that the foreign currency
can be sold for at least the strike price.

1.13

problems.promptEn: Practice 1.14. A U.S. company expects to have to pay 1 million Canadian dollars in 6 months. Explain how the exchange rate risk can be hedged using (a) a forward contract and (b) an option.
problems.promptZh: й—®йўҳ 1.14. дёҖе®¶зҫҺеӣҪе…¬еҸёйў„и®ЎйңҖиҰҒеңЁ6дёӘжңҲеҶ…ж”Ҝд»ҳ100дёҮеҠ е…ғгҖӮи§ЈйҮҠеҰӮдҪ•дҪҝз”Ё (a) иҝңжңҹеҗҲзәҰе’Ң (b) жңҹжқғеҜ№еҶІжұҮзҺҮйЈҺйҷ©гҖӮ
problems.answer: See the official worked solution below.
problems.answerEn: See the official worked solution below.
problems.explanation:
1.14
The company could enter into a long forward contract to buy 1 million Canadian dollars in
six months. This would have the effect of locking in an exchange rate equal to the current
forward exchange rate. Alternatively, the company could buy a call option giving it the right
(but not the obligation) to purchase 1 million Canadian dollars at a certain exchange rate in
six months. This would provide insurance against a strong Canadian dollar in six months
while still allowing the company to benefit from a weak Canadian dollar at that time.
problems.explanationEn:
1.14

(but not the obligation) to purchase 1 million Canadian dollars at a certain exchange rate in
six months. This would provide insurance against a strong Canadian dollar in six months
while still allowing the company to benefit from a weak Canadian dollar at that time.

problems.promptEn: Practice 1.19. In the 1980s, Bankers Trust developed index currency option notes (ICONs). These were bonds in which the amount received by the holder at maturity varied with a foreign exchange rate. One example was its trade with the Long Term Credit Bank of Japan. The ICON specified that if the yen/USD exchange rate, ST, is greater than 169 yen per dollar at maturity (in 1995), the holder of the bond receives USD 1,000. If it is less than 169 yen per dollar, the amount received by the holder of the bond is 1,000 - maxc 0, 1,000a169 ST - 1b d When the exchange rate is below 84.5, nothing is received by the holder at maturity. Show that this ICON is a combination of a regular bond and two options.

problems.promptEn: Practice 1.21. Suppose that USD/sterling spot and forward exchange rates are as follows: Spot 1.2580 90-day forward 1.2556 180-day forward 1.2518 What opportunities are open to an arbitrageur in the following situations? (a) A 180-day European call option to buy ВЈ1 for USD 1.22 costs 2 cents. (b) A 90-day European put option to sell ВЈ1 for USD 1.29 costs 2 cents.
problems.promptZh: й—®йўҳ 1.21. еҒҮи®ҫзҫҺе…ғ/иӢұй•‘еҚіжңҹе’ҢиҝңжңҹжұҮзҺҮеҰӮдёӢпјҡ еҚіжңҹ 1.2580 90 еӨ©иҝңжңҹ 1.2556 180 еӨ©иҝңжңҹ 1.2518 еңЁд»ҘдёӢжғ…еҶөдёӢпјҢеҘ—еҲ©иҖ…жңүе“Әдәӣжңәдјҡпјҹ (a) д»Ҙ\$1.22 costs 2 cents. (b) A 90-day European put option to sell ВЈ1 for \$1.29 зҡ„д»·ж јиҙӯд№° 1 иӢұй•‘зҡ„ 180 еӨ©ж¬§ејҸзңӢж¶Ёжңҹжқғзҡ„д»·ж јдёә 2 зҫҺеҲҶгҖӮ
problems.answer: See the official worked solution below.
problems.answerEn: See the official worked solution below.
problems.explanation:
1.21
(a) The arbitrageur buys a 180-day call option and takes a short position in a 180-day
forward contract. If
T
S is the terminal spot rate, the profit from the call option is
max(ST вҖ“ 1.22,0) вҖ“ 0.02

 The profit from the short forward contract is
1.2518 вҖ“ ST

 The profit from the strategy is therefore

max(ST вҖ“ 1.22,0) вҖ“ 0.02+1.2518 вҖ“ ST

This is
1.2318вҲ’ST when ST <1.22
0.0118
 when ST >1.22

This shows that the profit is always positive. The time value of money has been ignored
in these calculations. However, when it is taken into account the strategy is still likely to
be profitable in all circumstances. (We would require an extremely high interest rate for
USD 0.0118 interest to be required on an outlay of USD 0.02 over a 180-day period.)

(b) The trader buys 90-day put options and takes a long position in a 90 day forward
contract. If
T
S is the terminal spot rate, the profit from the put option is
max(1.29 вҖ“ ST, 0) вҖ“ 0.02

The profit from the long forward contract is
STвҲ’1.2556
The profit from this strategy is therefore
max(1.29 вҖ“ ST, 0) вҖ“ 0.02+ STвҲ’1.2556

This is
ST вҲ’1.2756 when ST >1.29
0.0144 when ST <1.29

The profit is therefore always positive. Again, the time value of money has been ignored
but is unlikely to affect the overall profitability of the strategy. (We would require interest
rates to be extremely high for USD 0.0144 interest to be required on an outlay of USD 0.02 over a
90-day period.)
problems.explanationEn:
1.21
(a) The arbitrageur buys a 180-day call option and takes a short position in a 180-day
forward contract. If
T
S is the terminal spot rate, the profit from the call option is
max(ST вҖ“ 1.22,0) вҖ“ 0.02

 The profit from the short forward contract is
1.2518 вҖ“ ST

 The profit from the strategy is therefore

max(ST вҖ“ 1.22,0) вҖ“ 0.02+1.2518 вҖ“ ST

This is
1.2318вҲ’ST when ST <1.22
0.0118
 when ST >1.22

This shows that the profit is always positive. The time value of money has been ignored
in these calculations. However, when it is taken into account the strategy is still likely to
be profitable in all circumstances. (We would require an extremely high interest rate for
USD 0.0118 interest to be required on an outlay of USD 0.02 over a 180-day period.)

(b) The trader buys 90-day put options and takes a long position in a 90 day forward
contract. If
T
S is the terminal spot rate, the profit from the put option is
max(1.29 вҖ“ ST, 0) вҖ“ 0.02

The profit from the long forward contract is
STвҲ’1.2556
The profit from this strategy is therefore
max(1.29 вҖ“ ST, 0) вҖ“ 0.02+ STвҲ’1.2556

This is
ST вҲ’1.2756 when ST >1.29
0.0144 when ST <1.29

The profit is therefore always positive. Again, the time value of money has been ignored
but is unlikely to affect the overall profitability of the strategy. (We would require interest
rates to be extremely high for USD 0.0144 interest to be required on an outlay of USD 0.02 over a
90-day period.)

problems.promptEn: Practice 1.22. A trader buys a call option with a strike price of USD 30 for USD 3. Does the trader ever exercise the option and lose money on the trade? Explain your answer.
problems.promptZh: й—®йўҳ 1.22. дәӨжҳ“иҖ…д№°е…Ҙжү§иЎҢд»·ж јдёә \$30 for \$3 зҡ„зңӢж¶ЁжңҹжқғгҖӮдәӨжҳ“иҖ…жҳҜеҗҰжӣҫиЎҢдҪҝжңҹжқғе№¶еңЁдәӨжҳ“дёӯдәҸжҚҹпјҹи§ЈйҮҠдҪ зҡ„зӯ”жЎҲгҖӮ
problems.answer: See the official worked solution below.
problems.answerEn: See the official worked solution below.
problems.explanation:
1.22
If the stock price is between USD 30 and USD 33 at option maturity the trader will exercise the
option, but lose money on the trade. Consider the situation where the stock price is USD 31. If the
trader exercises, she loses USD 2 on the trade. If she does not exercise she loses USD 3 on the trade.
It is clearly better to exercise than not exercise.

trader exercises, she loses USD 2 on the trade. If she does not exercise she loses USD 3 on the trade.
It is clearly better to exercise than not exercise.

problems.promptEn: Practice 1.23. A trader sells a put option with a strike price of USD 40 for USD 5. What is the traderвҖҷs maximum gain and maximum loss? How does your answer change if it is a call option?
problems.promptZh: й—®йўҳ 1.23. дәӨжҳ“иҖ…еҚ–еҮәжү§иЎҢд»·ж јдёә \$40 for \$5 зҡ„зңӢи·ҢжңҹжқғгҖӮдәӨжҳ“иҖ…зҡ„жңҖеӨ§ж”¶зӣҠе’ҢжңҖеӨ§жҚҹеӨұжҳҜеӨҡе°‘пјҹеҰӮжһңжҳҜзңӢж¶ЁжңҹжқғпјҢдҪ зҡ„зӯ”жЎҲдјҡеҰӮдҪ•еҸҳеҢ–пјҹ
problems.answer: See the official worked solution below.
problems.answerEn: See the official worked solution below.
problems.explanation:
1.23
The traderвҖҷs maximum gain from the put option is USD 5. The maximum loss is USD 35,
corresponding to the situation where the option is exercised and the price of the underlying
asset is zero. If the option were a call, the traderвҖҷs maximum gain would still be USD 5, but there
would be no bound to the loss as there is in theory no limit to how high the asset price could
rise.

would be no bound to the loss as there is in theory no limit to how high the asset price could
rise.

problems.promptEn: Practice 1.25. On May 21, 2020, as indicated in Table 1.2, the spot ask price of Apple stock is USD 316.50 and the ask price of a call option with a strike price of USD 320 and a maturity date of September is USD 21.70. A trader is considering two alternatives: buy 100 shares of the stock and buy 100 September call options. For each alternative, what is (a) the upfront cost, (b) the total gain if the stock price in September is USD 400, and (c) the total loss if the stock price in September is USD 300. Assume that the option is not exercised before September and positions are unwound at option maturity.
problems.promptZh: й—®йўҳ 1.25. 2020 е№ҙ 5 жңҲ 21 ж—ҘпјҢеҰӮиЎЁ 1.2 жүҖзӨәпјҢиӢ№жһңиӮЎзҘЁзҡ„зҺ°иҙ§иҰҒд»·дёә\$316.50 and the ask price of a call option with a strike price of \$320пјҢ9 жңҲеҲ°жңҹж—Ҙдёә\$21.70. A trader is considering two alternatives: buy 100 shares of the stock and buy 100 September call options. For each alternative, what is (a) the upfront cost, (b) the total gain if the stock price in September is \$400пјҢ(c) еҰӮжһң 9 жңҲиӮЎд»·дёә 300 зҫҺе…ғпјҢеҲҷжҖ»жҚҹеӨұгҖӮеҒҮи®ҫиҜҘжңҹжқғеңЁ 9 жңҲд№ӢеүҚжңӘиЎҢжқғпјҢдё”еӨҙеҜёеңЁжңҹжқғеҲ°жңҹж—¶е№ід»“гҖӮ
problems.answer: See the official worked solution below.
problems.answerEn: See the official worked solution below.
problems.explanation:
1.25
a) The upfront cost for the stock alternative is USD 31,650. The upfront cost for the option
alternative is USD 2,170.
b) The gain from the stock alternative is USD 40,000 вҲ’ USD 31,650 = USD 8,350. The total gain
from the option alternative is (USD 400 вҲ’ USD 320) Г— 100 вҲ’ USD 2,170 = USD 5,830.
c) The loss from the stock alternative is USD 31,650 вҲ’ USD 30,000 = USD 1,650. The loss from the
option alternative is USD 2,170.

problems.promptEn: Practice 1.26. What is arbitrage? Explain the arbitrage opportunity when the price of a dually listed mining company stock is USD 50 (USD) on the New York Stock Exchange and USD 60 (CAD) on the Toronto Stock Exchange. Assume that the exchange rate is such that 1 U.S. dollar equals 1.21 Canadian dollars. Explain what is likely to happen to prices as traders take advantage of this opportunity.
problems.promptZh: й—®йўҳ 1.26. д»Җд№ҲжҳҜеҘ—еҲ©пјҹи§ЈйҮҠеҪ“еӨҡдјҰеӨҡиҜҒеҲёдәӨжҳ“жүҖеҸҢйҮҚдёҠеёӮзҹҝдёҡе…¬еҸёиӮЎзҘЁд»·ж јдёә\$50 (USD) on the New York Stock Exchange and \$60пјҲеҠ е…ғпјүж—¶зҡ„еҘ—еҲ©жңәдјҡгҖӮеҒҮи®ҫжұҮзҺҮдёә 1 зҫҺе…ғзӯүдәҺ 1.21 еҠ е…ғгҖӮи§ЈйҮҠеҪ“дәӨжҳ“иҖ…еҲ©з”ЁиҝҷдёӘжңәдјҡж—¶д»·ж јеҸҜиғҪдјҡеҸ‘з”ҹд»Җд№ҲеҸҳеҢ–гҖӮ
problems.answer: See the official worked solution below.
problems.answerEn: See the official worked solution below.
problems.explanation:
1.26
Arbitrage involves carrying out two or more different trades to lock in a profit. In this case,
traders can buy shares on the TSX and sell them on the NYSE to lock in a USD profit of 50-
60/1.21=0.41 per share. As they do this, the NYSE price will fall and the TSX price will rise
so that the arbitrage opportunity disappears.

problems.promptEn: Practice 1.28. In March, a U.S. investor instructs a broker to sell one July put option contract on a stock. The stock price is USD 42 and the strike price is USD 40. The option price is USD 3. Explain what the investor has agreed to. Under what circumstances will the trade prove to be profitable? What are the risks?
problems.promptZh: й—®йўҳ 1.28. 3 жңҲд»ҪпјҢдёҖдҪҚзҫҺеӣҪжҠ•иө„иҖ…жҢҮзӨәз»ҸзәӘдәәеҮәе”®дёҖд»Ҫ 7 жңҲиӮЎзҘЁзңӢи·ҢжңҹжқғеҗҲзәҰгҖӮиӮЎд»·дёә\$42 and the strike price is \$40гҖӮжңҹжқғд»·ж јдёә 3 зҫҺе…ғгҖӮи§ЈйҮҠжҠ•иө„иҖ…еҗҢж„Ҹзҡ„еҶ…е®№гҖӮеңЁд»Җд№Ҳжғ…еҶөдёӢдәӨжҳ“дјҡжңүеҲ©еҸҜеӣҫпјҹжңүе“ӘдәӣйЈҺйҷ©пјҹ
problems.answer: See the official worked solution below.
problems.answerEn: See the official worked solution below.
problems.explanation:
1.28
The investor has agreed to buy 100 shares of the stock for USD 40 in July (or earlier) if the party
on the other side of the transaction chooses to sell. The trade will prove profitable if the
option is not exercised or if the stock price is above USD 37 at the time of exercise. The risk to
the investor is that the stock price plunges to a low level. For example, if the stock price
drops to USD 1 by July, the investor loses USD 3,600. This is because the put options are exercised
and USD 40 is paid for 100 shares when the value per share is USD 1. This leads to a loss of USD 3,900
which is only a little offset by the premium of USD 300 received for the options.

problems.promptEn: Practice 1.29. A U.S. company knows it will have to pay 3 million euros in three months. The current exchange rate is 1.1500 dollars per euro. Discuss how forward and options contracts can be used by the company to hedge its exposure.
problems.promptZh: й—®йўҳ 1.29. дёҖе®¶зҫҺеӣҪе…¬еҸёзҹҘйҒ“дёүдёӘжңҲеҗҺеҝ…йЎ»ж”Ҝд»ҳ 300 дёҮж¬§е…ғгҖӮзӣ®еүҚзҡ„жұҮзҺҮдёә 1.1500 зҫҺе…ғе…‘ж¬§е…ғгҖӮи®Ёи®әе…¬еҸёеҰӮдҪ•дҪҝз”Ёиҝңжңҹе’ҢжңҹжқғеҗҲзәҰжқҘеҜ№еҶІе…¶йЈҺйҷ©гҖӮ
problems.answer: See the official worked solution below.
problems.answerEn: See the official worked solution below.
problems.explanation:
1.29
The company could enter into a forward contract obligating it to buy 3 million euros in three
months for a fixed price (the forward price). The forward price will be close to but not
exactly the same as the current spot price of 1.1500. An alternative would be to buy a call
option giving the company the right but not the obligation to buy 3 million euros for a
particular exchange rate (the strike price) in three months. The use of a forward contract locks
in, at no cost, the exchange rate that will apply in three months. The use of a call option
provides, at a cost, insurance against the exchange rate being higher than the strike price.
problems.explanationEn:
1.29
The company could enter into a forward contract obligating it to buy 3 million euros in three
months for a fixed price (the forward price). The forward price will be close to but not
exactly the same as the current spot price of 1.1500. An alternative would be to buy a call
option giving the company the right but not the obligation to buy 3 million euros for a
particular exchange rate (the strike price) in three months. The use of a forward contract locks
in, at no cost, the exchange rate that will apply in three months. The use of a call option
provides, at a cost, insurance against the exchange rate being higher than the strike price.

problems.promptEn: Practice 1.31. The price of gold is currently USD 1,200 per ounce. The forward price for delivery in 1 year is USD 1,300 per ounce. An arbitrageur can borrow money at 3% per annum. What should the arbitrageur do? Assume that the cost of storing gold is zero and that gold provides no income.
problems.promptZh: й—®йўҳ 1.31. зӣ®еүҚйҮ‘д»·дёә \$1,200 per ounce. The forward price for delivery in 1 year is \$1,300 жҜҸзӣҺеҸёгҖӮеҘ—еҲ©иҖ…еҸҜд»Ҙд»ҘжҜҸе№ҙ 3% зҡ„еҲ©зҺҮеҖҹй’ұгҖӮеҘ—еҲ©иҖ…еә”иҜҘеҒҡд»Җд№ҲпјҹеҒҮи®ҫеӮЁеӯҳй»„йҮ‘зҡ„жҲҗжң¬дёәйӣ¶пјҢе№¶дё”й»„йҮ‘дёҚжҸҗдҫӣд»»дҪ•ж”¶е…ҘгҖӮ
problems.answer: See the official worked solution below.
problems.answerEn: See the official worked solution below.
problems.explanation:
1.31
The arbitrageur should borrow money to buy a certain number of ounces of gold today and
short forward contracts on the same number of ounces of gold for delivery in one year. This
means that gold is purchased for USD 1,200 per ounce and sold for USD 1,300 per ounce. Interest on
the borrowed funds will be 0.03Г—USD 1,200 or USD 36 per ounce. A profit of USD 64 per ounce will
therefore be made.
problems.explanationEn:
1.31
The arbitrageur should borrow money to buy a certain number of ounces of gold today and
short forward contracts on the same number of ounces of gold for delivery in one year. This
means that gold is purchased for USD 1,200 per ounce and sold for USD 1,300 per ounce. Interest on
the borrowed funds will be 0.03Г—USD 1,200 or USD 36 per ounce. A profit of USD 64 per ounce will
therefore be made.

problems.promptEn: Practice 1.32. On May 21, 2020, an investor owns 100 Apple shares. As indicated in Table 1.3, the share price is about USD 316 and a December put option with a strike price of USD 290 costs USD 21.30. The investor is comparing two alternatives to limit downside risk. The first involves buying one December put option contract with a strike price of USD 290. The second involves instructing a broker to sell the 100 shares as soon as AppleвҖҷs price reaches USD 290. Discuss the advantages and disadvantages of the two strategies.
problems.promptZh: й—®йўҳ 1.32. 2020 е№ҙ 5 жңҲ 21 ж—ҘпјҢдёҖеҗҚжҠ•иө„иҖ…жӢҘжңү 100 иӮЎиӢ№жһңиӮЎзҘЁгҖӮеҰӮиЎЁ 1.3 жүҖзӨәпјҢиӮЎд»·зәҰдёә\$316 and a December put option with a strike price of \$290 жҲҗжң¬\$21.30. The investor is comparing two alternatives to limit downside risk. The first involves buying one December put option contract with a strike price of \$290гҖӮз¬¬дәҢдёӘжҳҜжҢҮзӨәз»ҸзәӘдәәеңЁиӢ№жһңиӮЎд»·иҫҫеҲ° 290 зҫҺе…ғж—¶з«ӢеҚіеҮәе”® 100 иӮЎиӮЎзҘЁгҖӮи®Ёи®әдёӨз§Қзӯ–з•Ҙзҡ„дјҳзӮ№е’ҢзјәзӮ№гҖӮ
problems.answer: See the official worked solution below.
problems.answerEn: See the official worked solution below.
problems.explanation:
1.32
The second alternative involves what is known as a stop or stop-loss order. It costs nothing
and ensures that USD 29,000, or close to USD 29,000, is realized for the holding in the event the
stock price ever falls to USD 290. The put option costs USD 2,130 and guarantees that the holding can
be sold for USD 29,000 any time up to December. If the stock price falls marginally below USD 290
and then rises the option will not be exercised, but the stop-loss order will lead to the holding
being liquidated. There are some circumstances where the put option alternative leads to a
better outcome and some circumstances where the stop-loss order leads to a better outcome.
If the stock price ends up below USD 290, the stop-loss order alternative leads to a better
outcome because the cost of the option is avoided. If the stock price falls to USD 280 in
November and then rises to USD 350 by December, the put option alternative leads to a better
outcome. The investor is paying USD 2,130 for the chance to benefit from this second type of
outcome.

problems.promptEn: Practice 1.33. A bond issued by Standard Oil some time ago worked as follows. The holder received no interest. At the bondвҖҷs maturity the company promised to pay USD 1,000 plus an additional amount based on the price of oil at that time. The additional amount was equal to the product of 170 and the excess (if any) of the price of a barrel of oil at maturity over USD 25. The maximum additional amount paid was USD 2,550 (which corresponds to a price of USD 40 per barrel). Show that the bond is a combination of a regular bond, a long position in call options on oil with a strike price of USD 25, and a short position in call options on oil with a strike price of USD 40. Introduction

problems.promptEn: Practice 1.34. Suppose that in the situation of Table 1.1 a corporate treasurer said: вҖңI will have ВЈ1 million to sell in 6 months. If the exchange rate is less than 1.19, I want you to give me 1.19. If it is greater than 1.25, I will accept 1.25. If the exchange rate is between 1.19 and 1.25, I will sell the sterling for the exchange rate.вҖқ How could you use options to satisfy the treasurer?
problems.promptZh: й—®йўҳ 1.34. еҒҮи®ҫеңЁиЎЁ1.1зҡ„жғ…еҶөдёӢпјҢдёҖдҪҚдјҒдёҡиҙўеҠЎдё»з®ЎиҜҙпјҡвҖңжҲ‘е°ҶеңЁ6дёӘжңҲеҶ…еҮәе”®100дёҮиӢұй•‘гҖӮеҰӮжһңжұҮзҺҮдҪҺдәҺ1.19пјҢжҲ‘еёҢжңӣдҪ з»ҷжҲ‘1.19гҖӮеҰӮжһңеӨ§дәҺ1.25пјҢжҲ‘дјҡжҺҘеҸ—1.25гҖӮеҰӮжһңжұҮзҺҮеңЁ1.19е’Ң1.25д№Ӣй—ҙпјҢжҲ‘дјҡж №вҖӢвҖӢжҚ®жұҮзҺҮеҮәе”®иӢұй•‘гҖӮвҖқдҪ еҰӮдҪ•еҲ©з”ЁжңҹжқғжқҘж»Ўи¶іиҙўеҠЎдё»з®Ўзҡ„иҰҒжұӮпјҹ

1.34
You sell the treasurer a put option on GBP with a strike price of 1.19 and buy from the
treasurer a call option on GBP with a strike price of 1.25. Both options are on one million
pounds and have a maturity of six months. This is known as a range forward contract and is
discussed in Chapter 17.

problems.promptEn: Practice 2.1. Suppose that you enter into a short futures contract to sell July silver for USD 17.20 per ounce. The size of the contract is 5,000 ounces. The initial margin is USD 4,000, and the maintenance margin is USD 3,000. What change in the futures price will lead to a margin call? What happens if you do not meet the margin call?
problems.promptZh: й—®йўҳ 2.1. еҒҮи®ҫдҪ зӯҫи®ўдёҖд»Ҫз©әеӨҙжңҹиҙ§еҗҲзәҰпјҢд»ҘжҜҸзӣҺеҸё 17.20 зҫҺе…ғзҡ„д»·ж јеҚ–еҮә 7 жңҲдәӨеүІзҡ„зҷҪй“¶гҖӮеҗҲзәҰи§„жЁЎдёә 5,000 зӣҺеҸёпјҢеҲқе§ӢдҝқиҜҒйҮ‘дёә 4,000 зҫҺе…ғпјҢз»ҙжҢҒдҝқиҜҒйҮ‘дёә 3,000 зҫҺе…ғгҖӮжңҹиҙ§д»·ж јеҸ‘з”ҹеӨҡеӨ§еҸҳеҢ–дјҡеҜјиҮҙиҝҪеҠ дҝқиҜҒйҮ‘йҖҡзҹҘпјҹеҰӮжһңдҪ жңӘиғҪж»Ўи¶іиҝҪеҠ дҝқиҜҒйҮ‘иҰҒжұӮпјҢдјҡеҸ‘з”ҹд»Җд№Ҳпјҹ
problems.answer: See the official worked solution below.
problems.answerEn: See the official worked solution below.
problems.explanation:
2.1
There will be a margin call when USD 1,000 has been lost from the margin account. This will occur
when the price of silver increases by 1,000/5,000 = USD 0.20. The price of silver must therefore rise
to USD 17.40 per ounce for there to be a margin call. If the margin call is not met, your broker closes
out your position.
problems.explanationEn:
2.1
There will be a margin call when USD 1,000 has been lost from the margin account. This will occur
when the price of silver increases by 1,000/5,000 = USD 0.20. The price of silver must therefore rise
to USD 17.40 per ounce for there to be a margin call. If the margin call is not met, your broker closes
out your position.

problems.promptEn: Practice 2.2. Suppose that in September 2021 a company takes a long position in a contract on May 2022 crude oil futures. It closes out its position in March 2022. The futures price (per barrel) is USD 48.30 when it enters into the contract, USD 50.50 when it closes out its position, and USD 49.10 at the end of December 2021. One contract is for the delivery of 1,000 barrels. What is the companyвҖҷs total profit? When is it realized? How is it taxed if it is (a) a hedger and (b) a speculator? Assume that the company has a December 31 year end.
problems.promptZh: й—®йўҳ 2.2. еҒҮи®ҫдёҖе®¶е…¬еҸёеңЁ 2021 е№ҙ 9 жңҲжҢҒжңү 2022 е№ҙ 5 жңҲеҺҹжІ№жңҹиҙ§еҗҲзәҰзҡ„еӨҡеӨҙеӨҙеҜёгҖӮ 2022е№ҙ3жңҲе№ід»“пјҢе№ід»“ж—¶жңҹиҙ§д»·ж јпјҲжҜҸжЎ¶пјүдёә\$48.30 when it enters into the contract, \$50.50пјҢ2021е№ҙ12жңҲжң«жңҹиҙ§д»·ж јдёә49.10зҫҺе…ғгҖӮдёҖд»ҪеҗҲзәҰдәӨеүІ1000жЎ¶гҖӮе…¬еҸёзҡ„жҖ»еҲ©ж¶ҰжҳҜеӨҡе°‘пјҹд»Җд№Ҳж—¶еҖҷе®һзҺ°е‘ўпјҹеҰӮжһңжҳҜ (a) еҘ—жңҹдҝқеҖјиҖ…е’Ң (b) жҠ•жңәиҖ…пјҢеҰӮдҪ•еҫҒзЁҺпјҹеҒҮи®ҫиҜҘе…¬еҸёзҡ„е№ҙз»“ж—Ҙдёә 12 жңҲ 31 ж—ҘгҖӮ
problems.answer: See the official worked solution below.

problems.explanation:
2.2
The total profit is (USD 50.50 - USD 48.30) x 1,000 = USD 2,200. Of this (USD 49.10 - USD 48.30) x 1,000 or
USD 800 is realized on a day-by-day basis between September 2021 and December 31, 2021. A
further (USD 50.50 - USD 49.10) x 1,000 or USD 1,400 is realized on a day-by-day basis between January
1, 2022, and March 2022. A hedger would be taxed on the whole profit of USD 2,200 in 2022. A
speculator would be taxed on USD 800 in 2021 and USD 1,400 in 2022.

problems.promptEn: Practice 2.3. What does a stop order to sell at USD 2 mean? When might it be used? What does a limit order to sell at USD 2 mean? When might it be used?
problems.promptZh: й—®йўҳ 2.3. д»Ҙ 2 зҫҺе…ғд»·ж ји§ҰеҸ‘зҡ„жӯўжҚҹеҚ–еҮәи®ўеҚ•жҳҜд»Җд№Ҳж„ҸжҖқпјҹе®ғйҖҡеёёеңЁд»Җд№Ҳжғ…еҶөдёӢдҪҝз”Ёпјҹд»Ҙ 2 зҫҺе…ғйҷҗд»·еҚ–еҮәзҡ„и®ўеҚ•жҳҜд»Җд№Ҳж„ҸжҖқпјҹе®ғйҖҡеёёеңЁд»Җд№Ҳжғ…еҶөдёӢдҪҝз”Ёпјҹ
problems.answer: See the official worked solution below.
problems.answerEn: See the official worked solution below.
problems.explanation:
2.3.
A stop order to sell at USD 2 is an order to sell at the best available price once a price of USD 2 or less is
reached. It could be used to limit the losses from an existing long position. A limit order to sell at
USD 2 is an order to sell at a price of USD 2 or more. It could be used to instruct a broker that a short
position should be taken, providing it can be done at a price at least as favorable as USD 2.
problems.explanationEn:

USD 2 is an order to sell at a price of USD 2 or more. It could be used to instruct a broker that a short
position should be taken, providing it can be done at a price at least as favorable as USD 2.

problems.promptEn: Practice 2.4. What differences exist in the way prices are quoted in the foreign exchange futures market, the foreign exchange spot market, and the foreign exchange forward market?
problems.promptZh: й—®йўҳ 2.4. еӨ–жұҮжңҹиҙ§еёӮеңәгҖҒеӨ–жұҮзҺ°иҙ§еёӮеңәгҖҒеӨ–жұҮиҝңжңҹеёӮеңәзҡ„жҠҘд»·ж–№ејҸжңүдҪ•еҢәеҲ«пјҹ
problems.answer: See the official worked solution below.
problems.answerEn: See the official worked solution below.
problems.explanation:
2.4
In futures markets, prices are quoted as the number of U.S. dollars per unit of foreign currency.
Spot and forward rates are quoted in this way for the British pound, euro, Australian dollar, and
New Zealand dollar. For other major currencies, spot and forward rates are quoted as the number
of units of foreign currency per U.S. dollar.

of units of foreign currency per U.S. dollar.

problems.promptEn: Practice 2.5. The party with a short position in a futures contract sometimes has options as to the precise asset that will be delivered, where delivery will take place, when delivery will take place, and so on. Do these options increase or decrease the futures price? Explain your reasoning.
problems.promptZh: й—®йўҳ 2.5. жңҹиҙ§еҗҲзәҰз©әеӨҙж–№жңүж—¶еҸҜд»ҘйҖүжӢ©дәӨеүІзҡ„е…·дҪ“иө„дә§гҖҒдәӨеүІең°зӮ№гҖҒдәӨеүІж—¶й—ҙзӯүгҖӮиҝҷдәӣжңҹжқғдјҡеўһеҠ иҝҳжҳҜеҮҸе°‘жңҹиҙ§д»·ж јпјҹи§ЈйҮҠдҪ зҡ„жҺЁзҗҶгҖӮ
problems.answer: See the official worked solution below.
problems.answerEn: See the official worked solution below.
problems.explanation:
2.5
These options make the contract less attractive to the party with the long position and more
attractive to the party with the short position. They therefore tend to reduce the futures price.

problems.promptEn: Practice 2.6. Explain how margin accounts protect futures traders against the possibility of default.
problems.promptZh: й—®йўҳ 2.6. и§ЈйҮҠдҝқиҜҒйҮ‘иҙҰжҲ·еҰӮдҪ•дҝқжҠӨжңҹиҙ§дәӨжҳ“иҖ…е…ҚеҸ—иҝқзәҰзҡ„еҪұе“ҚгҖӮ
problems.answer: See the official worked solution below.
problems.answerEn: See the official worked solution below.
problems.explanation:
2.6
Margin is money deposited by a trader with his or her broker. It acts as a guarantee that the trader
can cover any losses on the futures contract. The balance in the margin account is adjusted daily

to reflect gains and losses on the futures contract. If losses lead to the balance in the margin
account falling below a certain level, the trader is required to deposit a further margin. This
system makes it unlikely that the trader will default. A similar system of margin accounts makes
it unlikely that the traderвҖҷs broker will default on the contract it has with the clearing house
member and unlikely that the clearing house member will default with the clearing house.
problems.explanationEn:

system makes it unlikely that the trader will default. A similar system of margin accounts makes
it unlikely that the traderвҖҷs broker will default on the contract it has with the clearing house
member and unlikely that the clearing house member will default with the clearing house.

problems.promptEn: Practice 2.7. A trader buys two July futures contracts on frozen orange juice concentrate. Each contract is for the delivery of 15,000 pounds. The current futures price is 160 cents per pound, the initial margin is USD 6,000 per contract, and the maintenance margin is USD 4,500 per contract. What price change would lead to a margin call? Under what circumstances could USD 2,000 be withdrawn from the margin account?
problems.promptZh: й—®йўҳ 2.7. дёҖдҪҚдәӨжҳ“е‘ҳиҙӯд№°дәҶдёӨд»Ҫ 7 жңҲеҶ·еҶ»жө“зј©ж©ҷжұҒжңҹиҙ§еҗҲзәҰгҖӮжҜҸд»ҪеҗҲеҗҢзҡ„дәӨиҙ§йҮҸдёә 15,000 иӢұй•‘гҖӮеҪ“еүҚжңҹиҙ§д»·ж јдёәжҜҸзЈ…160зҫҺеҲҶпјҢжҜҸд»ҪеҗҲзәҰеҲқе§ӢдҝқиҜҒйҮ‘дёә\$6,000 per contract, and the maintenance margin is \$4,500гҖӮд»Җд№Ҳд»·ж јеҸҳеҢ–дјҡеҜјиҮҙиҝҪеҠ дҝқиҜҒйҮ‘пјҹд»Җд№Ҳжғ…еҶөдёӢеҸҜд»Ҙд»ҺдҝқиҜҒйҮ‘иҙҰжҲ·жҸҗеҸ–\$2,000пјҹ
problems.answer: See the official worked solution below.
problems.answerEn: See the official worked solution below.
problems.explanation:
2.7
There is a margin call if more than USD 1,500 is lost on one contract. This happens if the futures
price of frozen orange juice falls by more than 10 cents to below 150 cents per pound. USD 2,000
can be withdrawn from the margin account if there is a gain on one contract of USD 1,000. This will
happen if the futures price rises by 6.67 cents to 166.67 cents per pound.
problems.explanationEn:

can be withdrawn from the margin account if there is a gain on one contract of USD 1,000. This will
happen if the futures price rises by 6.67 cents to 166.67 cents per pound.

problems.promptEn: Practice 2.8. Show that, if the futures price of a commodity is greater than the spot price during the delivery period, then there is an arbitrage opportunity. Does an arbitrage opportunity exist if the futures price is less than the spot price? Explain your answer.
problems.promptZh: й—®йўҳ 2.8. иЎЁжҳҺпјҢеҰӮжһңжҹҗз§Қе•Ҷе“Ғзҡ„жңҹиҙ§д»·ж јеңЁдәӨеүІжңҹй—ҙеӨ§дәҺзҺ°иҙ§д»·ж јпјҢеҲҷеӯҳеңЁеҘ—еҲ©жңәдјҡгҖӮеҰӮжһңжңҹиҙ§д»·ж јдҪҺдәҺзҺ°иҙ§д»·ж јпјҢжҳҜеҗҰеӯҳеңЁеҘ—еҲ©жңәдјҡпјҹи§ЈйҮҠдҪ зҡ„зӯ”жЎҲгҖӮ
problems.answer: See the official worked solution below.
problems.answerEn: See the official worked solution below.
problems.explanation:
2.8
If the futures price is greater than the spot price during the delivery period, an arbitrageur buys
the asset, shorts a futures contract, and makes delivery for an immediate profit. If the futures
price is less than the spot price during the delivery period, there is no similar perfect arbitrage
strategy. An arbitrageur can take a long futures position but cannot force immediate delivery of
the asset. The decision on when delivery will be made is made by the party with the short
position. Nevertheless companies interested in acquiring the asset may find it attractive to enter
into a long futures contract and wait for delivery to be made.
problems.explanationEn:
2.8

strategy. An arbitrageur can take a long futures position but cannot force immediate delivery of
the asset. The decision on when delivery will be made is made by the party with the short
position. Nevertheless companies interested in acquiring the asset may find it attractive to enter
into a long futures contract and wait for delivery to be made.

problems.promptEn: Practice 2.9. Explain the difference between a market-if-touched order and a stop order.
problems.promptZh: й—®йўҳ 2.9. и§ЈйҮҠи§ҰеҸҠеёӮд»·и®ўеҚ•е’ҢжӯўжҚҹи®ўеҚ•д№Ӣй—ҙзҡ„еҢәеҲ«гҖӮ
problems.answer: See the official worked solution below.
problems.answerEn: See the official worked solution below.
problems.explanation:
2.9
A market-if-touched order is executed at the best available price after a trade occurs at a
specified price or at a price more favorable than the specified price. A stop order is executed at
the best available price after there is a bid or offer at the specified price or at a price less
favorable than the specified price.
problems.explanationEn:
2.9
A market-if-touched order is executed at the best available price after a trade occurs at a
specified price or at a price more favorable than the specified price. A stop order is executed at
the best available price after there is a bid or offer at the specified price or at a price less
favorable than the specified price.

problems.promptEn: Practice 2.10. Explain what a stopвҖ“limit order to sell at 20.30 with a limit of 20.10 means.

2.10
A stop-limit order to sell at 20.30 with a limit of 20.10 means that as soon as there is a bid at
20.30, the contract should be sold providing this can be done at 20.10 or a higher price.

20.30, the contract should be sold providing this can be done at 20.10 or a higher price.

problems.promptEn: Practice 2.12. Explain why collateral requirements increased in the OTC market as a result of regulations introduced since the 2008 financial crisis.
problems.promptZh: й—®йўҳ 2.12. и§ЈйҮҠдёәд»Җд№ҲиҮӘ 2008 е№ҙйҮ‘иһҚеҚұжңәд»ҘжқҘеҮәеҸ°зҡ„жі•и§„еҜјиҮҙеңәеӨ–дәӨжҳ“еёӮеңәзҡ„жҠөжҠје“ҒиҰҒжұӮеўһеҠ гҖӮ
problems.answer: See the official worked solution below.
problems.answerEn: See the official worked solution below.
problems.explanation:
2.12
Regulations require most standard OTC transactions entered into between financial institutions
to be cleared by CCPs. These have initial and variation margin requirements similar to
exchanges. There is also a requirement that initial and variation margin be provided for most
bilaterally cleared OTC transactions between financial institutions.

As more transactions go through CCPs there will more netting. (Transactions with counterparty
A can be netted against transactions with counterparty B provided both are cleared through the
same CCP.) This will tend to offset the increase in collateral somewhat. However, it is expected
that on balance there will be collateral increases.
problems.explanationEn:
2.12
Regulations require most standard OTC transactions entered into between financial institutions
to be cleared by CCPs. These have initial and variation margin requirements similar to
exchanges. There is also a requirement that initial and variation margin be provided for most
bilaterally cleared OTC transactions between financial institutions.

As more transactions go through CCPs there will more netting. (Transactions with counterparty
A can be netted against transactions with counterparty B provided both are cleared through the
same CCP.) This will tend to offset the increase in collateral somewhat. However, it is expected
that on balance there will be collateral increases.

йҡҸзқҖжӣҙеӨҡдәӨжҳ“йҖҡиҝҮдёӯеӨ®еҜ№жүӢж–№иҝӣиЎҢжё…з®—пјҢдјҡеўһеҠ дәӨжҳ“зҡ„жҠөж¶ҲеҮҖйўқгҖӮпјҲдёҺдәӨжҳ“еҜ№жүӢ A зҡ„дәӨжҳ“еҸҜд»ҘдёҺдёҺдәӨжҳ“еҜ№жүӢ B зҡ„дәӨжҳ“жҠөж¶ҲпјҢеүҚжҸҗжҳҜдёӨиҖ…еқҮйҖҡиҝҮеҗҢдёҖдёӘдёӯеӨ®еҜ№жүӢж–№жё…з®—гҖӮпјүиҝҷе°ҶйғЁеҲҶжҠөж¶ҲжҠөжҠје“Ғзҡ„еўһеҠ гҖӮз„¶иҖҢпјҢйў„и®ЎжҖ»дҪ“дёҠжҠөжҠје“ҒиҰҒжұӮе°ҶжңүжүҖеўһеҠ гҖӮ

problems.promptEn: Practice 2.13. The forward price of the Swiss franc for delivery in 45 days is quoted as 1.1000. The futures price for a contract that will be delivered in 45 days is 0.9000. Explain these two quotes. Which is more favorable for a trader wanting to sell Swiss francs?

problems.promptEn: Practice 2.14. Suppose you call your broker and issue instructions to sell one July hogs contract. Describe what happens.
problems.promptZh: й—®йўҳ 2.14. еҒҮи®ҫжӮЁиҮҙз”өз»ҸзәӘдәәе№¶еҸ‘еҮәеҚ–еҮәдёҖд»ҪдёғжңҲз”ҹзҢӘеҗҲзәҰзҡ„жҢҮд»ӨгҖӮжҸҸиҝ°еҸ‘з”ҹдәҶд»Җд№ҲгҖӮ
problems.answer: See the official worked solution below.
problems.answerEn: See the official worked solution below.
problems.explanation:
2.14
Live hog futures are traded by the CME Group. The broker will request some initial margin. The
order will be relayed by telephone to your brokerвҖҷs trading desk on the floor of the exchange (or
to the trading desk of another broker). It will then be sent by messenger to a commission broker
who will execute the trade according to your instructions. Confirmation of the trade eventually
reaches you. If there are adverse movements in the futures price, your broker may contact you to
request additional margin.

problems.explanation:
2.15
Speculators are important market participants because they add liquidity to the market. However,
regulators generally only approve contracts when they are likely to be of interest to hedgers as
well as speculators.

problems.promptEn: Practice 2.16. What do you think would happen if an exchange started trading a contract in which the quality of the underlying asset was incompletely specified?
problems.promptZh: й—®йўҳ 2.16. еҰӮжһңдәӨжҳ“жүҖејҖе§ӢдәӨжҳ“ж Үзҡ„иө„дә§иҙЁйҮҸжңӘе®Ңе…ЁжҢҮе®ҡзҡ„еҗҲзәҰпјҢжӮЁи®ӨдёәдјҡеҸ‘з”ҹд»Җд№Ҳпјҹ
problems.answer: See the official worked solution below.
problems.answerEn: See the official worked solution below.
problems.explanation:
2.16
The contract would not be a success. Parties with short positions would hold their contracts until
delivery and then deliver the cheapest form of the asset. This might well be viewed by the party
with the long position as garbage! Once news of the quality problem became widely known no
one would be prepared to buy the contract. This shows that futures contracts are feasible only
when there are rigorous standards within an industry for defining the quality of the asset. Many
futures contracts have in practice failed because of the problem of defining quality.

problems.promptEn: Practice 2.17. вҖңWhen a futures contract is traded on the floor of the exchange, it may be the case that the open interest increases by one, stays the same, or decreases by one.вҖқ Explain this statement.
problems.promptZh: й—®йўҳ 2.17. вҖңеҪ“жңҹиҙ§еҗҲзәҰеңЁдәӨжҳ“жүҖеңәеҶ…дәӨжҳ“ж—¶пјҢжҢҒд»“йҮҸеҸҜиғҪдјҡеўһеҠ дёҖгҖҒдҝқжҢҒдёҚеҸҳжҲ–еҮҸе°‘дёҖгҖӮвҖқи§ЈйҮҠдёҖдёӢиҝҷдёӘиҜҙжі•гҖӮ
problems.answer: See the official worked solution below.
problems.answerEn: See the official worked solution below.
problems.explanation:
2.17
If both sides of the transaction are entering into a new contract, the open interest increases by
one. If both sides of the transaction are closing out existing positions, the open interest decreases
by one. If one party is entering into a new contract while the other party is closing out an existing
position, the open interest stays the same.
problems.explanationEn:
2.17
If both sides of the transaction are entering into a new contract, the open interest increases by
one. If both sides of the transaction are closing out existing positions, the open interest decreases
by one. If one party is entering into a new contract while the other party is closing out an existing
position, the open interest stays the same.

problems.promptEn: Practice 2.18. Suppose that, on October 24, 2022, a company sells one April 2023 live cattle futures contract. It closes out its position on January 21, 2023. The futures price (per pound) is 121.20 cents when it enters into the contract, 118.30 cents when it closes out its position, and 118.80 cents at the end of December 2022. One contract is for the delivery of 40,000 pounds of cattle. What is the total profit? How is it taxed if the company is (a) a hedger and (b) a speculator? Assume that the company has a December 31 year end.

problems.promptEn: Practice 2.19. A cattle farmer expects to have 120,000 pounds of live cattle to sell in 3 months. The live cattle futures contract traded by the CME Group is for the delivery of 40,000 pounds of cattle. How can the farmer use the contract for hedging? From the farmerвҖҷs viewpoint, what are the pros and cons of hedging?
problems.promptZh: й—®йўҳ 2.19. дёҖдҪҚе…»зүӣжҲ·йў„и®Ў 3 дёӘжңҲеҶ…еҸҜеҮәе”® 120,000 зЈ…жҙ»зүӣгҖӮиҠқе•ҶжүҖдәӨжҳ“зҡ„жҙ»зүӣжңҹиҙ§еҗҲзәҰз”ЁдәҺдәӨеүІ 40,000 зЈ…зүӣгҖӮеҶңжҲ·еҰӮдҪ•еҲ©з”ЁеҗҲзәҰиҝӣиЎҢеҘ—жңҹдҝқеҖјпјҹд»ҺеҶңж°‘зҡ„и§’еәҰжқҘзңӢпјҢеҘ—жңҹдҝқеҖјжңүе“ӘдәӣдјҳзӮ№е’ҢзјәзӮ№пјҹ
problems.answer: See the official worked solution below.
problems.answerEn: See the official worked solution below.
problems.explanation:
2.19
The farmer can short 3 contracts that have 3 months to maturity. If the price of cattle falls, the
gain on the futures contract will offset the loss on the sale of the cattle. If the price of cattle rises,
the gain on the sale of the cattle will be offset by the loss on the futures contract. Using futures
contracts to hedge has the advantage that the farmer can greatly reduce the uncertainty about the
price that will be received. Its disadvantage is that the farmer no longer gains from favorable
movements in cattle prices.

contracts to hedge has the advantage that the farmer can greatly reduce the uncertainty about the
price that will be received. Its disadvantage is that the farmer no longer gains from favorable
movements in cattle prices.

problems.promptEn: Practice 2.20. It is July 2021. A mining company has just discovered a small deposit of gold. It will take 6 months to construct the mine. The gold will then be extracted on a more or less continuous basis for 1 year. Futures contracts on gold are available with delivery months every 2 months from August 2021 to December 2022. Each contract is for the delivery of 100 ounces. Discuss how the mining company might use futures markets for hedging.
problems.promptZh: й—®йўҳ 2.20. зҺ°еңЁжҳҜ 2021 е№ҙ 7 жңҲгҖӮдёҖе®¶зҹҝдёҡе…¬еҸёеҲҡеҲҡеҸ‘зҺ°дәҶдёҖдёӘе°ҸйҮ‘зҹҝгҖӮиҜҘзҹҝе»әи®ҫйңҖиҰҒ6дёӘжңҲж—¶й—ҙгҖӮз„¶еҗҺпјҢй»„йҮ‘е°ҶеңЁдёҖе№ҙеҶ…жҲ–еӨҡжҲ–е°‘ең°иҝһз»ӯејҖйҮҮгҖӮй»„йҮ‘жңҹиҙ§еҗҲзәҰдәҺ2021е№ҙ8жңҲиҮі2022е№ҙ12жңҲжңҹй—ҙжҜҸдёӨдёӘжңҲжҸҗдҫӣдёҖж¬ЎдәӨеүІжңҲд»ҪпјҢжҜҸеј еҗҲзәҰдәӨеүІйҮҸдёә100зӣҺеҸёгҖӮи®Ёи®әзҹҝдёҡе…¬еҸёеҰӮдҪ•еҲ©з”Ёжңҹиҙ§еёӮеңәиҝӣиЎҢеҜ№еҶІгҖӮ
problems.answer: See the official worked solution below.
problems.answerEn: See the official worked solution below.
problems.explanation:
2.20
The mining company can estimate its production on a month by month basis. It can then short
futures contracts to lock in the price received for the gold. For example, if a total of 3,000 ounces
are expected to be produced in September 2022 and October 2022, the price received for this
production can be hedged by shorting 30 October 2022 contracts.

problems.promptEn: Practice 2.21. Explain how CCPs work. What are the advantages to the financial system of requiring CCPs to be used for all standardized derivatives transactions between financial institutions?
problems.promptZh: й—®йўҳ 2.21. и§ЈйҮҠ CCP еҰӮдҪ•иҝҗдҪңгҖӮиҰҒжұӮйҮ‘иһҚжңәжһ„д№Ӣй—ҙзҡ„жүҖжңүж ҮеҮҶеҢ–иЎҚз”ҹе“ҒдәӨжҳ“йғҪдҪҝз”Ё CCP еҜ№йҮ‘иһҚдҪ“зі»жңүд»Җд№ҲеҘҪеӨ„пјҹ
problems.answer: See the official worked solution below.
problems.answerEn: See the official worked solution below.
problems.explanation:
2.21
A CCP stands between the two parties in an OTC derivative transaction in much the same way
that a clearing house does for exchange-traded contracts. It absorbs the credit risk but requires
initial and variation margin from each side. In addition, CCP members are required to contribute
to a default fund. The advantage to the financial system is that there is a lot more collateral (i.e.,
margin) available and it is therefore much less likely that a default by one major participant in
the derivatives market will lead to losses by other market participants. The disadvantage is that
CCPs are replacing banks as the too-big-to-fail entities in the financial system.

problems.promptEn: Practice 2.22. Trader A enters into futures contracts to buy 1 million euros for 1.1 million dollars in three months. Trader B enters in a forward contract to do the same thing. The exchange rate (dollars per euro) declines sharply during the first two months and then increases for the third month to close at 1.1300. Ignoring daily settlement, what is the total profit of each trader? When the impact of daily settlement is taken into account, which trader has done better?
problems.promptZh: й—®йўҳ 2.22. дәӨжҳ“иҖ… A зӯҫи®ўжңҹиҙ§еҗҲзәҰпјҢеңЁдёүдёӘжңҲеҶ…д»Ҙ 110 дёҮзҫҺе…ғд№°е…Ҙ 100 дёҮж¬§е…ғгҖӮдәӨжҳ“иҖ… B зӯҫи®ўиҝңжңҹеҗҲзәҰжқҘеҒҡеҗҢж ·зҡ„дәӢжғ…гҖӮжұҮзҺҮпјҲзҫҺе…ғе…‘ж¬§е…ғпјүеңЁеүҚдёӨдёӘжңҲеӨ§е№…дёӢи·ҢпјҢз„¶еҗҺеңЁз¬¬дёүдёӘжңҲдёҠж¶ЁпјҢж”¶дәҺ 1.1300гҖӮдёҚиҖғиҷ‘жҜҸж—Ҙз»“з®—пјҢжҜҸдёӘдәӨжҳ“иҖ…зҡ„жҖ»еҲ©ж¶ҰжҳҜеӨҡе°‘пјҹеҪ“иҖғиҷ‘еҲ°жҜҸж—Ҙз»“з®—зҡ„еҪұе“Қж—¶пјҢе“ӘдёӘдәӨжҳ“иҖ…еҒҡеҫ—жӣҙеҘҪпјҹ
problems.answer: See the official worked solution below.
problems.answerEn: See the official worked solution below.
problems.explanation:
2.22
The total profit of each trader in dollars is 0.03Г—1,000,000 = 30,000. Trader BвҖҷs profit is realized
at the end of the three months. Trader AвҖҷs profit is realized day-by-day during the three months.
Substantial losses are made during the first two months and profits are made during the final
month. It is likely that Trader B has done better because Trader A had to finance its losses during
the first two months.

problems.promptEn: Practice 2.23. Explain what is meant by open interest. Why does the open interest usually decline during the month preceding the delivery month? On a particular day, there were 2,000 trades in a particular futures contract. This means that there were 2,000 buyers (going long) and 2,000 sellers (going short). Of the 2,000 buyers, 1,400 were closing out positions and 600 were entering into new positions. Of the 2,000 sellers, 1,200 were closing out positions and 800 were entering into new positions. What is the impact of the dayвҖҷs trading on open interest?
problems.promptZh: й—®йўҳ 2.23. и§ЈйҮҠдёҖдёӢжңӘе№ід»“еҗҲзәҰзҡ„еҗ«д№үгҖӮдёәд»Җд№ҲдәӨеүІжңҲеүҚдёҖдёӘжңҲзҡ„жҢҒд»“йҮҸйҖҡеёёдјҡдёӢйҷҚпјҹеңЁжҹҗдёҖеӨ©пјҢзү№е®ҡжңҹиҙ§еҗҲзәҰжңү 2,000 з¬”дәӨжҳ“гҖӮиҝҷж„Ҹе‘ізқҖжңү 2,000 еҗҚд№°е®¶пјҲеҒҡеӨҡпјүе’Ң 2,000 еҗҚеҚ–е®¶пјҲеҒҡз©әпјүгҖӮеңЁ 2,000 еҗҚд№°е®¶дёӯпјҢ1,400 еҗҚд№°е®¶е№ід»“пјҢ600 еҗҚд№°е®¶е»әж–°д»“гҖӮеңЁ 2,000 еҗҚеҚ–е®¶дёӯпјҢ1,200 еҗҚжӯЈеңЁе№ід»“пјҢ800 еҗҚжӯЈеңЁе»әж–°д»“гҖӮеҪ“ж—ҘдәӨжҳ“еҜ№жҢҒд»“йҮҸжңүдҪ•еҪұе“Қпјҹ
problems.answer: See the official worked solution below.
problems.answerEn: See the official worked solution below.
problems.explanation:
2.23
Open interest is the number of contract outstanding. Many traders close out their positions just
before the delivery month is reached. This is why the open interest declines during the month
preceding the delivery month. The open interest went down by 600. We can see this in two ways.
First, 1,400 shorts closed out and there were 800 new shorts. Second, 1,200 longs closed out and
there were 600 new longs.

problems.promptEn: Practice 2.24. A company enters into a short futures contract to sell 5,000 bushels of wheat for 750 cents per bushel. The initial margin is USD 3,000 and the maintenance margin is USD 2,000. What price change would lead to a margin call? Under what circumstances could USD 1,500 be with- drawn from the margin account?
problems.promptZh: й—®йўҳ 2.24. дёҖе®¶е…¬еҸёзӯҫи®ўдёҖд»Ҫз©әеӨҙжңҹиҙ§еҗҲзәҰпјҢд»ҘжҜҸи’ІејҸиҖі 750 зҫҺеҲҶзҡ„д»·ж јеҮәе”® 5,000 и’ІејҸиҖіе°ҸйәҰгҖӮеҲқе§ӢдҝқиҜҒйҮ‘дёә\$3,000 and the maintenance margin is \$2,000гҖӮд»Җд№Ҳд»·ж јеҸҳеҢ–дјҡеҜјиҮҙиҝҪеҠ дҝқиҜҒйҮ‘пјҹд»Җд№Ҳжғ…еҶөдёӢеҸҜд»Ҙд»ҺдҝқиҜҒйҮ‘иҙҰжҲ·дёӯжҸҗеҸ– 1,500 зҫҺе…ғпјҹ
problems.answer: See the official worked solution below.
problems.answerEn: See the official worked solution below.
problems.explanation:
2.24
There is a margin call if USD 1,000 is lost on the contract. This will happen if the price of wheat
futures rises by 20 cents from 750 cents to 770 cents per bushel. USD 1,500 can be withdrawn if the
futures price falls by 30 cents to 720 cents per bushel.
problems.explanationEn:
2.24

futures price falls by 30 cents to 720 cents per bushel.

problems.promptEn: Practice 2.25. Suppose that there are no storage costs for crude oil and the interest rate for borrowing or lending is 4% per annum. How could you make money if the June and December futures contracts for a particular year trade at USD 50 and USD 56, respectively?
problems.promptZh: й—®йўҳ 2.25. еҒҮи®ҫеҺҹжІ№жІЎжңүеӮЁеӯҳжҲҗжң¬пјҢеҖҹиҙ·еҲ©зҺҮдёәжҜҸе№ҙ 4%гҖӮеҰӮжһңзү№е®ҡе№ҙд»Ҫзҡ„ 6 жңҲе’Ң 12 жңҲжңҹиҙ§еҗҲзәҰеҲҶеҲ«д»Ҙ\$50 and \$56 дәӨжҳ“пјҢжӮЁеҰӮдҪ•иөҡй’ұпјҹ
problems.answer: See the official worked solution below.
problems.answerEn: See the official worked solution below.
problems.explanation:
2.25
You could go long one June oil contract and short one December contract. In June, you take
delivery of the oil borrowing USD 50 per barrel at 4% to meet cash outflows. The interest
accumulated in six months is about 50Г—0.04Г—1/2 or USD 1 per barrel. In December, the oil is sold for
USD 56 per barrel which is more than the USD 51 that has to be repaid on the loan. The strategy
therefore leads to a profit. Note that this profit is independent of the actual price of oil in June
and December. It will be slightly affected by the daily settlement procedures.

problems.promptEn: Practice 2.26. What position is equivalent to a long forward contract to buy an asset at K on a certain date and a put option to sell it for K on that date.
problems.promptZh: й—®йўҳ 2.26. д»Җд№ҲеӨҙеҜёзӣёеҪ“дәҺеңЁжҹҗдёҖж—Ҙжңҹд»Ҙ K д»·ж јиҙӯд№°иө„дә§зҡ„еӨҡеӨҙиҝңжңҹеҗҲзәҰе’ҢеңЁиҜҘж—Ҙжңҹд»Ҙ K д»·ж јеҮәе”®иө„дә§зҡ„зңӢи·ҢжңҹжқғгҖӮ
problems.answer: See the official worked solution below.
problems.answerEn: See the official worked solution below.
problems.explanation:
2.26
The long forward contract provides a payoff of ST вҲ’ K where ST is the asset price on the date and
K is the delivery price. The put option provides a payoff of max (KвҲ’ST, 0). If ST > K the sum of
the two payoffs is ST вҖ“ K. If ST < K the sum of the two payoffs is 0. The combined payoff is
therefore max (ST вҖ“ K, 0). This is the payoff from a call option. The equivalent position is
therefore a call option.
problems.explanationEn:
2.26

K is the delivery price. The put option provides a payoff of max (KвҲ’ST, 0). If ST > K the sum of
the two payoffs is ST вҖ“ K. If ST < K the sum of the two payoffs is 0. The combined payoff is
therefore max (ST вҖ“ K, 0). This is the payoff from a call option. The equivalent position is
therefore a call option.

problems.promptEn: Practice 2.27. A bankвҖҷs derivatives transactions with a counterparty are worth ++10 million to the bank and are cleared bilaterally. The counterparty has posted USD 10 million of cash collateral. What credit exposure does the bank have?
problems.promptZh: й—®йўҳ 2.27. й“¶иЎҢдёҺдәӨжҳ“еҜ№жүӢзҡ„иЎҚз”ҹе“ҒдәӨжҳ“еҜ№й“¶иЎҢжқҘиҜҙд»·еҖј++1000дёҮпјҢе№¶иҝӣиЎҢеҸҢиҫ№жё…з®—гҖӮдәӨжҳ“еҜ№жүӢе·ІжҸҗдҫӣ 1000 дёҮзҫҺе…ғзҡ„зҺ°йҮ‘жҠөжҠје“ҒгҖӮй“¶иЎҢжңүе“Әдәӣ
problems.answer: See the official worked solution below.
problems.answerEn: See the official worked solution below.
problems.explanation:
2.27
The counterparty may stop posting collateral and some time will then elapse before the bank is
able to close out the transactions. During that time, the transactions may move in the bankвҖҷs
favor, increasing its exposure. Note that the bank is likely to have hedged the transactions and
will incur a loss on the hedge if the transactions move in the bankвҖҷs favor. For example, if the
transactions change in value from USD 10 to USD 13 million after the counterparty stops posting

problems.promptEn: Practice 3.1. Explain what is meant by a perfect hedge. Does a perfect hedge always lead to a better outcome than an imperfect hedge? Explain your answer.
problems.promptZh: й—®йўҳ 3.1. и§ЈйҮҠдёҖдёӢе®ҢзҫҺеҜ№еҶІзҡ„еҗ«д№үгҖӮе®ҢзҫҺзҡ„еҜ№еҶІжҳҜеҗҰжҖ»жҳҜжҜ”дёҚе®ҢзҫҺзҡ„еҜ№еҶІеёҰжқҘжӣҙеҘҪзҡ„з»“жһңпјҹи§ЈйҮҠдҪ зҡ„зӯ”жЎҲгҖӮ
problems.answer: See the official worked solution below.
problems.answerEn: See the official worked solution below.
problems.explanation:
3.1
A perfect hedge is one that completely eliminates the hedgerвҖҷs risk. A perfect hedge does not
always lead to a better outcome than an imperfect hedge. It just leads to a more certain outcome.
Consider a company that hedges its exposure to the price of an asset. Suppose the assetвҖҷs price
movements prove to be favorable to the company. A perfect hedge totally neutralizes the
companyвҖҷs gain from these favorable price movements. An imperfect hedge, which only partially
neutralizes the gains, might well give a better outcome.
problems.explanationEn:

neutralizes the gains, might well give a better outcome.

problems.promptEn: Practice 3.4. A company has a USD 20 million portfolio with a beta of 1.2. It would like to use futures contracts on a stock index to hedge its risk. The index futures price is currently standing at 1080, and each contract is for delivery of USD 250 times the index. What is the hedge that minimizes risk? What should the company do if it wants to reduce the beta of the portfolio to 0.6?
problems.promptZh: й—®йўҳ 3.4. дёҖе®¶е…¬еҸёзҡ„\$20 million portfolio with a beta of 1.2. It would like to use futures contracts on a stock index to hedge its risk. The index futures price is currently standing at 1080, and each contract is for delivery of $жҳҜиҜҘжҢҮж•°зҡ„250еҖҚгҖӮд»Җд№ҲжҳҜжңҖе°ҸеҢ–йЈҺйҷ©зҡ„еҜ№еҶІпјҹеҰӮжһңе…¬еҸёжғіе°ҶжҠ•иө„з»„еҗҲзҡ„иҙқеЎ”еҖјйҷҚдҪҺеҲ°0.6пјҢиҜҘжҖҺд№ҲеҠһпјҹ
problems.answer: See the official worked solution below.
problems.answerEn: See the official worked solution below.
problems.explanation:
3.4
The formula for the number of contracts that should be shorted gives

1.2 Г— 20,000,000
1,080 Г— 250 = 88.9

Rounding to the nearest whole number, 89 contracts should be shorted. To reduce the beta to 0.6,
half of this position, or a short position in 44 contracts, is required.

problems.promptEn: Practice 3.5. In the corn futures contract traded on an exchange, the following delivery months are available: March, May, July, September, and December. Which of the available contracts should be used for hedging when the expiration of the hedge is in (a) June, (b) July, and (c) January.
problems.promptZh: й—®йўҳ 3.5. еңЁдәӨжҳ“жүҖдәӨжҳ“зҡ„зҺүзұіжңҹиҙ§еҗҲзәҰдёӯпјҢжңүд»ҘдёӢдәӨеүІжңҲд»Ҫпјҡ3жңҲгҖҒ5жңҲгҖҒ7жңҲгҖҒ9жңҲе’Ң12жңҲгҖӮеҪ“еҘ—жңҹдҝқеҖјеҲ°жңҹж—Ҙдёә (a) 6 жңҲгҖҒ(b) 7 жңҲе’Ң (c) 1 жңҲж—¶пјҢеә”дҪҝз”Ёе“Әз§ҚеҸҜз”ЁеҗҲзәҰиҝӣиЎҢеҘ—жңҹдҝқеҖјгҖӮ
problems.answer: See the official worked solution below.
problems.answerEn: See the official worked solution below.
problems.explanation:
3.5
A good rule of thumb is to choose a futures contract that has a delivery month as close as
possible to, but later than, the month containing the expiration of the hedge. The contracts that
should be used are therefore,
(a) July
(b) September
(c) March

problems.promptEn: Practice 3.6. Does a perfect hedge always succeed in locking in the current spot price of an asset for a future transaction? Explain your answer.
problems.promptZh: й—®йўҳ 3.6. е®ҢзҫҺзҡ„еҜ№еҶІжҳҜеҗҰжҖ»иғҪжҲҗеҠҹй”Ғе®ҡиө„дә§зҡ„еҪ“еүҚзҺ°иҙ§д»·ж јд»ҘдҫӣжңӘжқҘдәӨжҳ“пјҹи§ЈйҮҠдҪ зҡ„зӯ”жЎҲгҖӮ

problems.answerEn: See the official worked solution below.

3.6
No. Consider, for example, the use of a forward contract to hedge a known cash inflow in a
foreign currency. The forward contract locks in the forward exchange rateвҖ”which is in general
different from the spot exchange rate.

problems.promptEn: Practice 3.7. Explain why a short hedgerвҖҷs position improves when the basis strengthens unexpectedly and worsens when the basis weakens unexpectedly.
problems.promptZh: й—®йўҳ 3.7. и§ЈйҮҠдёәд»Җд№ҲеҪ“еҹәе·®ж„ҸеӨ–иө°ејәж—¶з©әеӨҙеҘ—жңҹдҝқеҖјиҖ…зҡ„еӨҙеҜёдјҡж”№е–„пјҢиҖҢеҪ“еҹәе·®ж„ҸеӨ–иө°ејұж—¶з©әеӨҙеҘ—жңҹдҝқеҖјиҖ…зҡ„еӨҙеҜёдјҡжҒ¶еҢ–гҖӮ
problems.answer: See the official worked solution below.
problems.answerEn: See the official worked solution below.
problems.explanation:
3.7
The basis is the amount by which the spot price exceeds the futures price. A short hedger is long
the asset and short futures contracts. The value of his or her position therefore improves as the
basis increases. Similarly, it worsens as the basis decreases.
problems.explanationEn:
3.7

the asset and short futures contracts. The value of his or her position therefore improves as the
basis increases. Similarly, it worsens as the basis decreases.

еҹәе·®жҳҜжҢҮзҺ°иҙ§д»·ж ји¶…иҝҮжңҹиҙ§д»·ж јзҡ„йҮ‘йўқгҖӮз©әеӨҙеҘ—жңҹдҝқеҖјиҖ…жҢҒжңүзҡ„жҳҜиө„дә§зҡ„еӨҡеӨҙеӨҙеҜёе’Ңжңҹиҙ§еҗҲзәҰзҡ„з©әеӨҙеӨҙеҜёгҖӮеӣ жӯӨпјҢеҪ“еҹәе·®еўһеҠ ж—¶пјҢд»–жҲ–еҘ№зҡ„еӨҙеҜёд»·еҖјдјҡж”№е–„гҖӮеҗҢж ·пјҢеҪ“еҹәе·®еҮҸе°‘ж—¶пјҢеӨҙеҜёд»·еҖјдјҡжҒ¶еҢ–гҖӮ

problems.promptEn: Practice 3.8. Imagine you are the treasurer of a Japanese company exporting electronic equipment to the United States. Discuss how you would design a foreign exchange hedging strategy and the arguments you would use to sell the strategy to your fellow executives.
problems.promptZh: й—®йўҳ 3.8. жғіиұЎдёҖдёӢпјҢжӮЁжҳҜдёҖе®¶еҗ‘зҫҺеӣҪеҮәеҸЈз”өеӯҗи®ҫеӨҮзҡ„ж—Ҙжң¬е…¬еҸёзҡ„иҙўеҠЎдё»з®ЎгҖӮи®Ёи®әжӮЁе°ҶеҰӮдҪ•и®ҫи®ЎеӨ–жұҮеҜ№еҶІзӯ–з•ҘпјҢд»ҘеҸҠжӮЁе°Ҷз”ЁжқҘеҗ‘е…¶д»–й«ҳз®ЎжҺЁй”ҖиҜҘзӯ–з•Ҙзҡ„и®әжҚ®гҖӮ
problems.answer: See the official worked solution below.
problems.answerEn: See the official worked solution below.
problems.explanation:
3.8
The simple answer to this question is that the treasurer should:
1. Estimate the companyвҖҷs future cash flows in Japanese yen and U.S. dollars.
2. Enter into forward and futures contracts to lock in the exchange rate for the U.S. dollar
cash flows.
However, this is not the whole story. As the gold jewelry example in Table 3.1 shows, the
company should examine whether the magnitudes of the foreign cash flows depend on the
exchange rate. For example, will the company be able to raise the price of its product in U.S.
dollars if the yen appreciates? If the company can do so, its foreign exchange exposure may be
quite low. The key estimates required are those showing the overall effect on the companyвҖҷs
profitability of changes in the exchange rate at various times in the future. Once these estimates
have been produced, the company can choose between using futures and options to hedge its
risk. The results of the analysis should be presented carefully to other executives. It should be
explained that a hedge does not ensure that profits will be higher. It means that profit will be
more certain. When futures/forwards are used, both the downside and upside are eliminated.
With options, a premium is paid to eliminate only the downside.
problems.explanationEn:

problems.promptEn: Practice 3.9. Suppose that in Example 3.2 of Section 3.3 the company decides to use a hedge ratio of 0.8. How does the decision affect the way in which the hedge is implemented and the result?
problems.promptZh: й—®йўҳ 3.9. еҒҮи®ҫеңЁз¬¬ 3.3 иҠӮзҡ„зӨәдҫӢ 3.2 дёӯпјҢе…¬еҸёеҶіе®ҡдҪҝз”Ё 0.8 зҡ„еҜ№еҶІжҜ”зҺҮгҖӮиҜҘеҶіе®ҡеҰӮдҪ•еҪұе“ҚеҜ№еҶІзҡ„е®һж–Ҫж–№ејҸе’Ңз»“жһңпјҹ
problems.answer: See the official worked solution below.
problems.answerEn: See the official worked solution below.
problems.explanation:
3.9
If the hedge ratio is 0.8, the company takes a long position in 16 December oil futures contracts
on June 8 when the futures price is USD 48.00. It closes out its position on November 10. The spot
price and futures price at this time are USD 50.00 and USD 49.10. The gain on the futures position is
(49.10 вҲ’ 48.00) Г— 16,000 = 17,600
The effective cost of the oil is therefore
20,000 Г— 50 вҖ“ 17,600 = 982, 400
or USD 49.12 per barrel. (This compares with USD 48.90 per barrel when the company is fully hedged.)
problems.explanationEn:
3.9

on June 8 when the futures price is USD 48.00. It closes out its position on November 10. The spot
price and futures price at this time are USD 50.00 and USD 49.10. The gain on the futures position is
(49.10 вҲ’ 48.00) Г— 16,000 = 17,600
The effective cost of the oil is therefore
20,000 Г— 50 вҖ“ 17,600 = 982, 400
or USD 49.12 per barrel. (This compares with USD 48.90 per barrel when the company is fully hedged.)

problems.promptEn: Practice 3.11. вҖңIf there is no basis risk, the minimum variance hedge ratio is always 1.0.вҖқ Is this statement true? Explain your answer.
problems.promptZh: й—®йўҳ 3.11. вҖңеҰӮжһңжІЎжңүеҹәе·®йЈҺйҷ©пјҢжңҖе°Ҹж–№е·®еҜ№еҶІжҜ”зҺҮе§Ӣз»Ҳдёә 1.0гҖӮвҖқиҝҷдёӘиҜҙжі•еұһе®һеҗ—пјҹи§ЈйҮҠдҪ зҡ„зӯ”жЎҲгҖӮ
problems.answer: See the official worked solution below.
problems.answerEn: See the official worked solution below.
problems.explanation:
3.11
The statement is true. Suppose for the sake of definiteness that the commodity is being purchased. If the
hedge ratio is h, the gain on futures is h(F2 вҖ“F1) so that the price paid is S2 вҲ’ h(F2 вҖ“F1) or
 hb2 + hF1+(1вҲ’h)S2. If there is no basis risk, b2 is known. For a given h, there is therefore no uncertainty
in the first two terms. For any value of h other than 1, there is uncertainty in the third term. The minimum
variance hedge ratio is therefore 1.
problems.explanationEn:
3.11

hedge ratio is h, the gain on futures is h(F2 вҖ“F1) so that the price paid is S2 вҲ’ h(F2 вҖ“F1) or
 hb2 + hF1+(1вҲ’h)S2. If there is no basis risk, b2 is known. For a given h, there is therefore no uncertainty
in the first two terms. For any value of h other than 1, there is uncertainty in the third term. The minimum
variance hedge ratio is therefore 1.

The standard deviation of monthly changes in the futures price of live cattle for the closest contract is 1.4. The correlation between the futures price changes and the spot price changes is 0.7. It is now October 15. A beef producer is committed to purchasing 200,000 pounds of live cattle on November 15. The producer wants to use the December live cattle futures contracts to hedge its risk. Each contract is for the delivery of 40,000 pounds of cattle. What strategy should the beef producer follow?

problems.promptEn: Practice 3.14. A corn farmer argues вҖңI do not use futures contracts for hedging. My real risk is not the price of corn. It is that my whole crop gets wiped out by the weather.вҖқ Discuss this viewpoint. Should the farmer estimate his or her expected production of corn and hedge to try to lock in a price for expected production?
problems.promptZh: й—®йўҳ 3.14. дёҖдҪҚзҺүзұіз§ҚжӨҚиҖ…иҫ©з§°пјҢвҖңжҲ‘дёҚдҪҝз”Ёжңҹиҙ§еҗҲзәҰиҝӣиЎҢеҘ—жңҹдҝқеҖјгҖӮжҲ‘зңҹжӯЈзҡ„йЈҺйҷ©дёҚжҳҜзҺүзұід»·ж јгҖӮиҖҢжҳҜжҲ‘зҡ„е…ЁйғЁдҪңзү©йғҪиў«еӨ©ж°”жҜҒжҺүдәҶгҖӮвҖқи®Ёи®әдёҖдёӢиҝҷдёӘи§ӮзӮ№гҖӮеҶңж°‘жҳҜеҗҰеә”иҜҘдј°и®Ўд»–жҲ–еҘ№зҡ„зҺүзұійў„жңҹдә§йҮҸе№¶иҝӣиЎҢеҘ—жңҹдҝқеҖјд»Ҙе°қиҜ•й”Ғе®ҡйў„жңҹдә§йҮҸзҡ„д»·ж јпјҹ
problems.answer: See the official worked solution below.
problems.answerEn: See the official worked solution below.
problems.explanation:
3.14
If weather creates a significant uncertainty about the volume of corn that will be harvested, the
farmer should not enter into short forward contracts to hedge the price risk on the expected
production. The reason is as follows. Suppose that the weather is bad and the farmerвҖҷs
production is lower than expected. Other farmers are likely to have been affected similarly. Corn
production overall will be low and as a consequence the price of corn will be relatively high. The
farmerвҖҷs problems arising from the bad harvest will be made worse by losses on the short futures
position. This problem emphasizes the importance of looking at the big picture when hedging.
The farmer is correct to question whether hedging price risk while ignoring other risks is a good
strategy.
problems.explanationEn:

position. This problem emphasizes the importance of looking at the big picture when hedging.
The farmer is correct to question whether hedging price risk while ignoring other risks is a good
strategy.

problems.promptEn: Practice 3.15. On July 1, an investor holds 50,000 shares of a certain stock. The market price is USD 30 per share. The investor is interested in hedging against movements in the market over the next month and decides to use an index futures contract. The index futures price is currently 1,500 and one contract is for delivery of USD 50 times the index. The beta of the stock is 1.3. What strategy should the investor follow? Under what circumstances will it be profitable?

problems.promptEn: Practice 3.17. An airline executive has argued: вҖңThere is no point in our using oil futures. There is just as much chance that the price of oil in the future will be less than the futures price as there is that it will be greater than this price.вҖқ Discuss the executiveвҖҷs viewpoint.
problems.promptZh: й—®йўҳ 3.17. дёҖдҪҚиҲӘз©әе…¬еҸёй«ҳз®Ўиҫ©з§°пјҡвҖңжҲ‘д»¬дҪҝз”ЁзҹіжІ№жңҹиҙ§жІЎжңүд»»дҪ•ж„Ҹд№үгҖӮжңӘжқҘзҹіжІ№д»·ж јдҪҺдәҺжңҹиҙ§д»·ж јзҡ„еҸҜиғҪжҖ§дёҺй«ҳдәҺжңҹиҙ§д»·ж јзҡ„еҸҜиғҪжҖ§дёҖж ·еӨ§гҖӮвҖқи®Ёи®әдё»з®Ўзҡ„и§ӮзӮ№гҖӮ
problems.answer: See the official worked solution below.
problems.answerEn: See the official worked solution below.
problems.explanation:
3.17
It may well be true that there is just as much chance that the price of oil in the future will be
above the futures price as it will be below the futures price. This means that the use of a futures
contract for speculation would be like betting on whether a coin comes up heads or tails. But it
might make sense for the airline to use futures for hedging rather than speculation. The futures
contract then has the effect of reducing risks. It can be argued that an airline should not expose
its shareholders to risks associated with the future price of oil when there are contracts available
to hedge the risks.

its shareholders to risks associated with the future price of oil when there are contracts available
to hedge the risks.

problems.promptEn: Practice 3.18. Suppose that the 1-year gold lease rate is 1.5% and the 1-year risk-free rate is 5.0%. Both rates are compounded annually. Use the discussion in Business Snapshot 3.1 to calculate the maximum 1-year gold forward price Goldman Sachs should quote to the gold-mining company when the spot price is USD 1,200.
problems.promptZh: й—®йўҳ 3.18. еҒҮи®ҫ1е№ҙжңҹй»„йҮ‘з§ҹиөҒеҲ©зҺҮдёә1.5%пјҢ1е№ҙжңҹж— йЈҺйҷ©еҲ©зҺҮдёә5.0%гҖӮдёӨз§ҚеҲ©зҺҮеқҮжҢүе№ҙеӨҚеҲ©и®Ўз®—гҖӮдҪҝз”ЁдёҡеҠЎеҝ«з…§ 3.1 дёӯзҡ„и®Ёи®әжқҘи®Ўз®—еҪ“зҺ°иҙ§д»·ж јдёә 1,200 зҫҺе…ғж—¶й«ҳзӣӣеә”еҗ‘йҮ‘зҹҝе…¬еҸёжҠҘд»·зҡ„жңҖй«ҳ 1 е№ҙжңҹй»„йҮ‘иҝңжңҹд»·ж јгҖӮ
problems.answer: See the official worked solution below.
problems.answerEn: See the official worked solution below.
problems.explanation:
3.18
Goldman Sachs can borrow 1 ounce of gold and sell it for USD 1200. It invests the USD 1,200 at 5% so
that it becomes USD 1,260 at the end of the year. It must pay the lease rate of 1.5% on USD 1,200. This
is USD 18 and leaves it with USD 1,242. It follows that if it agrees to buy the gold for less than USD 1,242 in
one year it will make a profit.

problems.promptEn: Practice 3.20. It is now June. A company knows that it will sell 5,000 barrels of crude oil in September. It uses the October CME Group futures contract to hedge the price it will receive. Each contract is on 1,000 barrels of вҖңlight sweet crude.вҖқ What position should it take? What price risks is it still exposed to after taking the position?
problems.promptZh: й—®йўҳ 3.20. зҺ°еңЁжҳҜе…ӯжңҲгҖӮдёҖе®¶е…¬еҸёзҹҘйҒ“ 9 жңҲд»Ҫе°ҶеҮәе”® 5,000 жЎ¶еҺҹжІ№гҖӮе®ғдҪҝз”ЁиҠқе•ҶжүҖеҚҒжңҲжңҹиҙ§еҗҲзәҰжқҘеҜ№еҶІе…¶е°Ҷж”¶еҲ°зҡ„д»·ж јгҖӮжҜҸд»ҪеҗҲзәҰйғҪеҢ…еҗ« 1,000 жЎ¶вҖңиҪ»иҙЁдҪҺзЎ«еҺҹжІ№вҖқгҖӮеә”иҜҘйҮҮеҸ–д»Җд№Ҳз«Ӣеңәпјҹе»әд»“еҗҺиҝҳйқўдёҙе“Әдәӣд»·ж јйЈҺйҷ©пјҹ
problems.answer: See the official worked solution below.
problems.answerEn: See the official worked solution below.
problems.explanation:
3.20
It should short five contracts. It has basis risk. It is exposed to the difference between the
October futures price and the spot price of light sweet crude at the time it closes out its position
in September. It is also possibly exposed to the difference between the spot price of light sweet
crude and the spot price of the type of oil it is selling.

problems.promptEn: Practice 3.21. Sixty futures contracts are used to hedge an exposure to the price of silver. Each futures contract is on 5,000 ounces of silver. At the time the hedge is closed out, the basis is USD 0.20 per ounce. What is the effect of the basis on the hedgerвҖҷs financial position if (a) the trader is hedging the purchase of silver and (b) the trader is hedging the sale of silver?
problems.promptZh: й—®йўҳ 3.21. е…ӯеҚҒд»Ҫжңҹиҙ§еҗҲзәҰз”ЁдәҺеҜ№еҶІзҷҪй“¶д»·ж јйЈҺйҷ©гҖӮжҜҸд»Ҫжңҹиҙ§еҗҲзәҰзҡ„зҷҪй“¶еҗ«йҮҸдёә 5,000 зӣҺеҸёгҖӮеҜ№еҶІе№ід»“ж—¶пјҢеҹәе·®дёәжҜҸзӣҺеҸё 0.20 зҫҺе…ғгҖӮеҰӮжһң (a) дәӨжҳ“иҖ…еҜ№зҷҪй“¶зҡ„иҙӯд№°иҝӣиЎҢеҜ№еҶІпјҢе№¶дё” (b) дәӨжҳ“иҖ…еҜ№зҷҪй“¶зҡ„й”Җе”®иҝӣиЎҢеҜ№еҶІпјҢйӮЈд№Ҳеҹәе·®еҜ№еҘ—жңҹдҝқеҖјиҖ…зҡ„иҙўеҠЎзҠ¶еҶөжңүдҪ•еҪұе“Қпјҹ
problems.answer: See the official worked solution below.
problems.answerEn: See the official worked solution below.
problems.explanation:
3.21
The excess of the spot over the futures at the time the hedge is closed out is USD 0.20 per ounce. If
the trader is hedging the purchase of silver, the price paid is the futures price plus the basis. The
trader therefore loses 60 Г— 5,000 Г— USD 0.20 = USD 60,000. If the trader is hedging the sales of silver,
the price received is the futures price plus the basis. The trader therefore gains USD 60,000.
problems.explanationEn:

the trader is hedging the purchase of silver, the price paid is the futures price plus the basis. The
trader therefore loses 60 Г— 5,000 Г— USD 0.20 = USD 60,000. If the trader is hedging the sales of silver,
the price received is the futures price plus the basis. The trader therefore gains USD 60,000.

problems.promptEn: Practice 3.22. A trader owns 55,000 units of a particular asset and decides to hedge the value of her position with futures contracts on another related asset. Each futures contract is on 5,000 units. The spot price of the asset that is owned is USD 28 and the standard deviation of the change in this price over the life of the hedge is estimated to be USD 0.43. The futures price of the related asset is USD 27 and the standard deviation of the change in this over the life of the hedge is USD 0.40. The coefficient of correlation between the spot price change and futures price change is 0.95. (a) What is the minimum variance hedge ratio? (b) Should the hedger take a long or short futures position? (c) What is the optimal number of futures contracts when adjustments for daily settle- ment are not considered? (d) How can the daily settlement of futures contracts be taken into account?
problems.promptZh: й—®йўҳ 3.22. дёҖдҪҚдәӨжҳ“иҖ…жӢҘжңү 55,000 еҚ•дҪҚзҡ„зү№е®ҡиө„дә§пјҢе№¶еҶіе®ҡз”ЁеҸҰдёҖз§Қзӣёе…іиө„дә§зҡ„жңҹиҙ§еҗҲзәҰеҜ№еҶІе…¶еӨҙеҜёд»·еҖјгҖӮжҜҸд»Ҫжңҹиҙ§еҗҲзәҰзҡ„ж•°йҮҸдёә 5,000 дёӘеҚ•дҪҚгҖӮжүҖжӢҘжңүиө„дә§зҡ„зҺ°иҙ§д»·ж јдёә\$28 and the standard deviation of the change in this price over the life of the hedge is estimated to be \$0.43гҖӮзӣёе…іиө„дә§зҡ„жңҹиҙ§д»·ж јдёә\$27 and the standard deviation of the change in this over the life of the hedge is \$0.40гҖӮзҺ°иҙ§д»·ж јеҸҳеҢ–дёҺжңҹиҙ§д»·ж јеҸҳеҢ–д№Ӣй—ҙзҡ„зӣёе…ізі»ж•°дёә0.95гҖӮ (a) жңҖе°Ҹж–№е·®еҜ№еҶІжҜ”зҺҮжҳҜеӨҡе°‘пјҹ (b) еҘ—жңҹдҝқеҖјиҖ…еә”иҜҘжҢҒжңүеӨҡеӨҙиҝҳжҳҜз©әеӨҙжңҹиҙ§еӨҙеҜёпјҹ (c) еңЁдёҚиҖғиҷ‘жҜҸж—Ҙз»“з®—и°ғж•ҙзҡ„жғ…еҶөдёӢпјҢжңҖдҪіжңҹиҙ§еҗҲзәҰж•°йҮҸжҳҜеӨҡе°‘пјҹ (d) еҰӮдҪ•иҖғиҷ‘жңҹиҙ§еҗҲзәҰзҡ„жҜҸж—Ҙз»“з®—пјҹ еҲ©з”Ёжңҹиҙ§зҡ„еҜ№еҶІзӯ–з•Ҙ

problems.promptEn: Practice 3.23. A company wishes to hedge its exposure to a new fuel whose price changes have a 0.6 correlation with gasoline futures price changes. The company will lose USD 1 million for each 1 cent increase in the price per gallon of the new fuel over the next three months. The new fuelвҖҷs price changes have a standard deviation that is 50% greater than price changes in gasoline futures prices. If gasoline futures are used to hedge the exposure, what should the hedge ratio be? What is the companyвҖҷs exposure measured in gallons of the new fuel? What position, measured in gallons, should the company take in gasoline futures? How many gasoline futures contracts should be traded? Each contract is on 42,000 gallons.
